source.name: AstroGuide bundled equipment catalog
source.notes: Wraps the bundled smart telescope and filter catalog in the dynamic metadata package envelope.

items.name: Seestar S50

built_in_filters.name: UV/IR Cut
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in Seestar broadband UV/IR-cut style filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as a built-in Seestar broadband UV/IR-cut style filter. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

built_in_filters.name: Dark Frame Calibration
built_in_filters.notes: Represents Seestar's built-in dark-frame calibration capability; included for completeness even though it is not an optical filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Dark-frame calibration is represented for equipment completeness, but it is not an optical bandpass filter.

built_in_filters.name: Dual-band LP
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in Seestar dual-band light-pollution / nebula mode filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as the built-in Seestar dual-band LP/nebula filter class. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

items.notes: Built-in filter set is published; limiting magnitude varies heavily with sky brightness and stacking duration.

power.notes: Manufacturer says up to 6 hours per charge and supports recharging with a phone power bank while in use.

items.notes: Tele lens POV used (device also has a wide lens). Includes solar filter accessory; limiting magnitude estimated.

power.notes: Modeled like Seestar S50/S30-class devices; verify exact S30 Pro spec before treating as published.

items.name: Seestar S30 Pro Wide
items.notes: Wide-angle camera POV for Milky Way, star trail, constellation, and landscape-style framing. Modeled separately until smart telescope imaging modes can be represented under one device. Official wide camera spec lists a 63-degree diagonal field of view, IMX586 sensor, and 6mm f/1.75 lens; tele-path built-in filters are not modeled for this wide profile.

power.notes: Uses the same S30 Pro body and battery model as the tele profile; exact wide-mode runtime varies by capture mode.

items.name: DWARF (original)
items.notes: Legacy model; I did not find a clean canonical, current spec sheet for FOV/filters. Max magnitude is a family-class estimate.

power.notes: Legacy model; runtime is an estimate due to lack of a clean current canonical spec.

items.name: DWARF II

built_in_filters.name: Mode-based imaging modes
built_in_filters.notes: Vendor describes imaging modes more clearly than a discrete optical built-in filter set.
built_in_filters.source_notes: DWARF original mode behavior is not modeled as a discrete optical filter; bandpass depends on selected imaging mode and device pipeline.

items.notes: Tele camera POV; device also includes a wide camera. Limiting magnitude estimated.

power.notes: Short-session smart scope; use conservative runtime for planning.

items.name: DWARF 3

built_in_filters.name: VIS
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in broadband visible-light mode/filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as DWARF visible-light broadband capture. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

built_in_filters.name: Astro
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in astro imaging mode/filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as DWARF astro broadband capture mode. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

built_in_filters.name: Dual-band
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in dual-band filter/mode for emission targets.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as DWARF built-in dual-band emission mode/filter. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

items.notes: POV derived from published tele lens/sensor class; limiting magnitude estimated.

power.notes: Published/commonly reported battery class is 10000 mAh with roughly 4 hours; external USB-C power can extend use.

items.name: Vespera (original)

built_in_filters.name: Optional filter ecosystem
built_in_filters.notes: Vaonis positions filters as optional accessories rather than an always-present built-in filter set.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Vaonis optional filters are modeled as accessory compatibility here; actual bandpass depends on the installed user filter.

items.notes: Limiting magnitude estimated; Vaonis typically emphasizes optional filter accessories rather than built-in filter switching.

power.notes: Vaonis lists 8h normal / 6h with dew heater; external USB-C PD battery supported.

items.name: Stellina

built_in_filters.name: CLS
built_in_filters.notes: Built-in CLS-style light-pollution filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Modeled as Stellina built-in CLS-style broadband light-pollution filter. Exact device transmission curve was not harvested; values are planning heuristics.

items.notes: Older Vaonis smartscope; filter swapping not positioned as a built-in system like Seestar.

power.notes: Older Vaonis smart telescope; modeled as long-session device. Verify exact battery spec before treating as published.

items.name: Hestia

built_in_filters.name: Phone-dependent
built_in_filters.best_for: Varies by phone and accessory configuration
built_in_filters.notes: Hestia relies heavily on phone-camera behavior rather than a discrete built-in filter set.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Hestia capture characteristics depend on the attached phone camera and accessories rather than a fixed built-in telescope filter.

items.notes: Vaonis lists limiting magnitude as ~7–8; this file stores midpoint 7.5. Performance varies strongly by phone.

power.notes: No telescope battery; session duration depends on phone battery, phone model, screen use, temperature, and external phone power.

items.name: eVscope (original)

built_in_filters.name: Software LP suppression
built_in_filters.notes: Software processing feature rather than a physical optical filter.
built_in_filters.source_notes: Unistellar light-pollution suppression is modeled as software processing, not a physical optical bandpass filter.

items.notes: FOV commonly cited as 27×37 arcmin; limiting magnitude varies with conditions.

power.notes: Unistellar Expert range lists up to 10h; can be used while charging, but standard 5V/2.4A charging is recommended.

items.name: L-eXtreme
items.notes: Classic “city nebula” dual-band pick.
items.source_notes: Optolong describes L-eXtreme as dual 7nm Ha/OIII bandpass with strong light-pollution blocking.

items.name: L-eNhance
items.notes: Wider passband than L-eXtreme; still no SII.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.name: L-Synergy
items.notes: 7nm SII/OIII dual-band filter; designed to pair with L-eXtreme for OSC SHO workflows.
items.source_notes: Optolong describes L-Synergy as OIII 7nm + SII 7nm dual-narrowband with high transmission and out-of-band rejection.

items.name: L-Ultimate
items.notes: Ultra-narrow class variant exists.
items.source_notes: Modeled as Optolong ultra-narrow Ha/OIII dual-band; exact vended curve should be verified separately.

items.name: L-Pro
items.notes: Not line-isolating.
items.source_notes: Optolong describes L-Pro as a multi-bandpass broadband LP filter with low reduction for continuous-spectrum objects and high transmission at major nebula lines.

items.name: L-Quad
items.bands_purpose: Hβ + OIII + Hα + SII with partial continuum/star-color preservation
items.best_for: Emission nebulae with improved star color retention; mixed emission + starfield targets (OSC)
items.notes: Hybrid quad-band behavior: passes key nebular lines including SII, but should be modeled separately for line availability vs isolation. More continuum-friendly than L-eXtreme/L-Ultimate; less moon-resistant than ultra-narrow dual-band filters.
items.source_notes: Optolong lists L-QEF/L-Quad as >90% transmittance at H-Beta/OIII/H-Alpha/SII with OD2–OD4 cut-off depth.

items.name: ALP-T
items.notes: Often chosen to reduce halos vs some alternatives.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.name: D1
items.notes: Commonly cross-shopped in dual-band category.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.notes: Same family as D1; popular alternative.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.notes: Premium dual-band option.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.name: Triad
items.notes: “True” tri-band includes SII.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.notes: Narrower tri-band variant.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.name: Moon & Skyglow (Neodymium)
items.notes: Contrast/LP shaping; not narrowband.

items.name: UHC / UHC-S
items.notes: UHC-style passband, not explicit Ha/OIII narrow passbands.

items.name: UHC
items.notes: UHC-style, not explicit line-only.
items.source_notes: No manufacturer transmission page harvested yet; values remain planning heuristics.

items.name: SV240
items.notes: Multi-band OSC nebula filter (no SII).
items.source_notes: SVBONY describes SV240 as multi-narrowband isolating Ha, OIII, and H-Beta from background light pollution.

items.name: SV260
items.notes: Not a line-isolation filter.
items.source_notes: SVBONY describes SV260 as a 5-bandpass broadband LP filter with OD4 cut-off and over 90% peak transmittance.

items.name: SV220 (Ha+OIII, 7nm)
items.notes: SVBONY dual-band “nebula” filter variant.
items.source_notes: SVBONY describes SV220 Ha/OIII 7nm as dual narrowband; product listings cite high/90%+ peak transmission and OD5 cut-off.

items.name: SV220 (Ha+OIII, 3nm)
items.notes: Ultra-narrow dual-band variant.
items.source_notes: SVBONY describes SV220 Ha/OIII 3nm as 3±1nm dual narrowband with OD5 blocking across 300–1050nm; listings cite >85% peak transmission.

items.name: SV220 (SII+OIII, 7nm)
items.notes: Distinct SV220 variant (not Ha/OIII).
items.source_notes: SVBONY describes SV220 SII/OIII 7nm as >85% transmission at 500.7nm and 672nm with broad background rejection.

items.notes: UHC-style; not explicit line passbands.

schema_notes.has_fields_semantics: has_HA/has_OIII/has_SII mean the filter isolates that line strongly enough to plan as emission-line capture, not merely that the wavelength passes.
schema_notes.documented_transmission_semantics: documented_transmission contains raw/normalized pass and block details, including lines that are available but not isolated.
